mainEntity.name: St. George hospital worker exposed to disinfectants—do I have to choose between comp or lawsuit?

acceptedAnswer.text: No, you can do both. Workers' comp provides immediate benefits from your employer, while a separate lawsuit can be filed against the chemical manufacturer or an outside contractor if their negligence caused your exposure.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Yes, you do not have to choose; however, workers' compensation is your only path for benefits from your employer. If the disinfectant was defective or used improperly by an outside cleaning contractor, you could pursue a third-party lawsuit simultaneously. This allowed you to get immediate medical help from workers' comp while seeking larger damages for pain and suffering from the third party.

How to handle both:
- File the workers\' comp claim first to ensure your doctor bills are paid.
- Identify the brand and manufacturer of the disinfectant.
- Check if other healthcare workers have sued over the same product.

The hospital may try to claim the disinfectant is "safe," but medical-grade cleaners are often highly toxic if not diluted correctly. You need a lawyer to see if the hospital's equipment (like miscalibrated dilution pumps) or the chemical manufacturer is at fault. This dual approach maximizes your total recovery.
